w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Confidential – For Internal and Stakeholder Use Only

This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) provides a comprehensive technical and functional overview of the Smart LMS Platform. It serves as the Single Source of Truth for Version 1.0 development and guides developers, project managers, QA testers, and stakeholders in delivering an AI-powered, scalable learning management solution.
Developers – Backend (Laravel/PHP) and Frontend (Next.js) engineers responsible for implementation.
Product Owners – To validate feature alignment with target market segments (institutes, corporates, tutors).
QA Testers – To derive comprehensive test cases from defined functional requirements.
Investors & Stakeholders – To understand the technical architecture, moat, and scalability roadmap.
This document governs development of Version 1.0 of the Smart LMS. It defines the scope boundary between core Phase 1 LMS features and future Phase 2 marketplace and API capabilities.
platform serving both B2B and B2B2C models. It provides:
Centralized course delivery and content management across multiple institutes.
White-labeling capabilities enabling institutes to operate under their own brand.
Role-based access control for Admins, Teachers, and Students.
The platform is designed to eliminate manual grading burdens and fragmented content delivery across educational institutes, corporate training departments, and independent tutors.
software platform for managing educational courses and training.
Architecture where a single software instance serves multiple distinct client organizations (tenants).
Business-to-Business-to-Consumer – selling to businesses who then serve end consumers.
Annual Contract Value – total revenue from a subscription contract over one year.
Row-Level Security – PostgreSQL feature restricting data access at the database row level.
Failure Mode and Effects Analysis – systematic risk assessment methodology.
General Data Protection Regulation – EU data privacy law.
Digital Personal Data Protection Act – India's data privacy legislation.
Application Programming Interface – protocol for software components to communicate.

Smart LMS is a greenfield SaaS product positioned as an all-in-one learning management solution. It interfaces with third-party services including OpenAI for AI assessment, Stripe for payment processing, SendGrid for transactional email, and Agora/
for live video classrooms. The platform replaces fragmented tooling (separate LMS + quiz tools + video conferencing) with a unified, white-labeled experience.
Centralize course content and automate assessments for student populations ranging from 50 to 5,000.
Support multi-branch management under a single institutional account.
Enable teachers to generate AI-powered quizzes directly from uploaded PDFs and notes.
Track employee compliance certification completion across departments.
Assign role-based training paths for onboarding and ongoing professional development.
Generate audit-ready compliance reports for HR and legal teams.
Launch personalized online courses with custom domains within 30 minutes.
Scale student enrollments from 10 to 1,000 using affordable subscription tiers.
Access white-labeled branding tools without technical expertise.
The platform will be deployed exclusively on AWS infrastructure (EC2 for
, RDS for managed PostgreSQL, S3 for media storage).
All API integrations (OpenAI, Stripe, SendGrid, Agora/
) will maintain their documented SLAs and versioned APIs.
Client devices will support modern browsers (Chrome 90+, Firefox 90+, Safari 14+) with JavaScript enabled.
Static assets and edge video delivery
When a new institute completes registration, the system shall automatically provision a logically isolated environment including:
A dedicated schema or row-level security policy in PostgreSQL scoped to that tenant.
Custom branding configuration (logo, color palette, domain/subdomain mapping).
Default role setup (Admin, Teacher, Student) with pre-configured permissions.
A white-labeled login page accessible via the institute's custom subdomain.
Admins and Teachers shall be able to create, organize, and publish courses consisting of video lectures, documents, quizzes, and assignments.
The system shall support structured course hierarchies: Course > Module > Lesson.
Content uploads shall support PDF, MP4, DOCX, and image formats with server-side validation.
Teachers shall be able to schedule content release dates (drip content).
When a student passes an assessment based on configurable passing criteria, the system shall automatically generate a branded certificate.
When a teacher uploads a PDF or text note, the system shall invoke the OpenAI API to generate
up to 50 multiple-choice, short-answer, or true/false questions.
When a student submits an essay or long-form answer, the system shall use AI to evaluate the response against
and return a score with written feedback within 30 seconds.
Teachers shall be able to review, edit, or reject AI-generated questions before publishing.
The system shall maintain a fallback question bank for AI service disruptions.
The platform shall integrate Agora or
SDK to support live video classroom sessions of up to 500 concurrent participants.
Teachers shall be able to schedule, host, and record live sessions from within the platform.
Students shall receive automated email and in-app reminders 24 hours and 1 hour before a scheduled session.
Certificates generated from assessments shall be downloadable as PDF, shareable through secure links, and revocable by tenant administrators.
The system shall track individual student and employee course completion, quiz scores, and time-on-platform metrics.
Admins shall be able to export compliance reports in CSV and PDF formats.
Automated certification generation shall be triggered upon successful course completion.
The platform shall support three subscription tiers: Starter, Growth, and Professional (see Section 5.1 for tier details).
Stripe shall process all subscription payments with automated monthly
If an institute exceeds its allocated student seat limit, the system shall apply an automatic overage fee of $2–$5 per additional student per month.
Admins shall receive automated email notifications at 80% and 100% of seat limit utilization.
All API endpoints in the Laravel backend shall enforce JWT-based authentication and role-based authorization (Admin, Teacher, Student).
Sensitive data shall be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3).
PostgreSQL row-level security policies shall prevent cross-tenant data access at the database layer.
The system shall undergo automated OWASP vulnerability scanning on every production deployment.
GDPR and DPDP compliance shall be maintained including right-to-erasure and data export APIs.
The PostgreSQL database shall utilize schema-based or row-level security multi-tenancy to support 500+ institutes without performance degradation.
The architecture shall support horizontal scaling of Laravel API servers via AWS Auto Scaling Groups.
and media assets shall be served via AWS CloudFront CDN with regional edge caching.
1 million total enrolled students across all tenants.
The platform shall maintain a minimum uptime SLA of 99.5% per calendar month.
Database backups shall be taken every 6 hours with a 30-day retention period.
Deployment pipelines shall include automated health checks and zero-downtime rollout strategies.
All user-facing interfaces shall be built using Next.js with responsive design supporting desktop (1280px+), tablet (768px), and mobile (375px) viewports.
The student and teacher dashboards shall load within 2 seconds on a 4G mobile connection.
The platform shall comply with WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards.
AI question generation and essay evaluation
The PostgreSQL database shall implement multi-tenancy using one of the following approaches, evaluated during the architecture design phase:
Schema-Based Isolation: Each institute is assigned a dedicated PostgreSQL schema, providing strong logical separation.
Row-Level Security (RLS): A shared schema with RLS policies filtering all queries by a
column, offering better resource efficiency at scale.
Regardless of approach, the database design shall ensure zero possibility of cross-tenant data leakage at the query level.
GDPR Compliance: Student personal data stored for EU users shall comply with GDPR including lawful basis for processing, right to erasure, and data portability.
DPDP Compliance: Data of Indian users shall comply with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023.
Data Residency: Enterprise tier clients shall be offered data residency options (EU or APAC regions) upon request.
Audit Logging: All administrative actions and data access events shall be logged with user identity, timestamp, and IP address for a minimum of 12 months.
Inadequate multi-tenant isolation in DB or API layer
Strict RLS policies at DB level; automated security regression tests on every deploy.
OpenAI API outage or rate-limit breach
Implement local fallback question bank; cache recent AI outputs; monitor API quotas.
Stripe API downtime or webhook failure
retry logic; webhook verification; manual override by admin.
Automated 6-hour backups; point-in-time recovery enabled on AWS RDS.
Quarterly legal review; data processing agreements with all third-party vendors.
Use Case 4: Automated Certificate Generation by Assessment
Actor: Student | Trigger: Student passes an assessment
System generates branded certificate with QR verification.
Admin verifies or revokes certificate if required
Actor: Teacher | Trigger: Teacher uploads a PDF lecture note
Teacher navigates to Course > Lesson > Add Assessment.
Teacher uploads a PDF file (max 20MB) and selects question type (MCQ, True/False, Short Answer) and count (up to 50).
System invokes OpenAI API to analyze the document and generate questions.
AI-generated questions appear in the teacher's review queue within 60 seconds.
Teacher reviews, edits any questions, and publishes the
Actor: HR Manager | Trigger: End of compliance certification period
HR Manager logs in to the Admin Dashboard and
Manager selects a certification course and filters by department or role.
System displays a completion matrix showing percentage complete per employee.
Manager exports the report as PDF or CSV for regulatory submission.
System sends automated reminder emails to employees who have not completed the certification.
Use Case 3: Solo Tutor Course Launch
Actor: Independent Tutor | Trigger: Tutor signs up for the Professional
Tutor registers and selects the Professional subscription plan.
System provisions a white-labeled subdomain (e.g., tutorname.smartlms.io) within 5 minutes.
Tutor uploads a course banner, sets custom colors, and adds a logo.
Tutor creates a course with modules, lessons, and a pricing page.
Tutor shares the course link; students can enroll and pay via integrated Stripe checkout.
Entire onboarding and launch process completes in under 30 minutes.
Added non-functional requirements, FMEA, and API interfaces.
Final review, legal requirements, and use cases added. Approved for development.
